p.em: Demultiplexer → Dispatcher → Handler Table → Concrete Handlers

div.p: Linux Kernel NIC interrupt → DMA → socket receive buffer → epoll readiness queue entry added → epoll_wait() wakes calling thread Event sources: TCP data, EOF, RST, send-buffer drain

Pending Ops Queue (re-entrancy safety) pending_mod_t pending_ops[256] During dispatch (dispatching=1): reactor_register → enqueues PENDING_ADD/MOD reactor_deregister → enqueues PENDING_DEL, sets closing=1 Phase 2 (after dispatch): applies all ops via epoll_ctl Phase 2 clears handler slots for PENDING_DEL
Boundary Invariant User code (green zone) contains zero epoll_* symbols. All kernel interaction is mediated by the reactor (blue zone). The orange deferred queue makes callbacks re-entrancy-safe.

ol.li: Translate epoll flags → REACTOR_READ | WRITE | ERROR

ul.li: Sets idle + header timers via reactor_set_timeout
REACTOR_READ → drain loop until EAGAIN → write(fd, buf, n)
Resets idle timer on each read() > 0

div.p: http_io_callback reactor_io_cb — HTTP/1.1 state machine dispatcher

ul.li: REACTOR_WRITE → http_handle_write(conn) → write_buf drain → EPOLLOUT lifecycle
